transcript: Houses and shops were set on fire in various parts of Manipur late on Wednesday. The incident happened after a massive rally organized by the All Tribal Students Union Manipur turned violent in areas bordering Bishnupur and Churachandpur districts. The rally was organized in protest of the inclusion of Mete community in the Scheduled Tribe category following an April 19 Manipur High Court directive. A rally organized in Churachandpur district late on Wednesday turned violent when the demonstrators clashed with a group of people in an area bordering the Bishnupur district. According to reports, some unidentified miscreants set fire to houses belonging to a particular group. However, there is no official confirmation of the details of any casualties. Following the incident, many other incidents of violence were reported across the northeastern state, including the state capital, Imphal. After the violence, the state government imposed a curfew in the majority of 16 districts until further order. Internet services were also suspended in the area for the next five days. In Imphal city, there were several reports of homes belonging to the tribal residents being attacked. The police resorted to tear gas shelling in most parts of Imphal till late night. The fresh incident of violence in the state comes just days after Chudha Chandpur District witnessed violence following Chief Minister N. Biren Singh's planned visit to the district to inaugurate an open gym.
